title: Beyond code generation: rethinking engineering productivity in the age of AI agents
author: Kazuaki Okumura
summary: How Dropbox is moving from AI tools that assist engineers to agentic systems that can execute scoped tasks, and how we’re building platforms to support those workflows.

title: Introducing Nova, our internal platform for coding agents
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler
summary: Nova lets engineers run multiple coding sessions in parallel and lets internal systems use AI agents as part of automated workflows.

title: Improving storage efficiency in Magic Pocket, our immutable blob store
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel
summary: By turning compaction into a layered, adaptive pipeline and strengthening our monitoring and controls, we made Magic Pocket more resilient to workload changes.

title: Reducing our monorepo size to improve developer velocity
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra
summary: Monorepos will continue to grow as products evolve, but growth doesn’t have to mean friction.

title: How we optimized Dash's relevance judge with DSPy
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra,Eric Wang,Dmitriy Meyerzon
summary: We used DSPy to turn prompt engineering for our relevance judge into a measurable, automated optimization loop, improving task performance, cost, and how reliably it works in production.

title: Using LLMs to amplify human labeling and improve Dash search relevance
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra,Eric Wang,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Dmitriy Meyerzon
summary: How we train Dash's search ranking models with a mix of human and LLM-assisted labeling.

title: How low-bit inference enables efficient AI
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra,Eric Wang,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Hicham Badri,Appu Shaji
summary: Making products like Dropbox Dash accessible to individuals and businesses means tackling new challenges around efficiency and resource use.

title: Insights from our executive roundtable on AI and engineering productivity
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra,Eric Wang,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Hicham Badri,Appu Shaji,Craig Wilhite
summary: From Claude Code to Cursor, we're big adopters of AI coding tools at Dropbox. The early results have been promising, but there are still a lot of open questions about how to work with these tools most effectively and where they can have the most impact. To push this conversation forward, we hosted an executive roundtable at our San Francisco…

title: Engineering VP Josh Clemm on how we use knowledge graphs, MCP, and DSPy in Dash
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra,Eric Wang,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Hicham Badri,Appu Shaji,Craig Wilhite,Josh Clemm
summary: Engineering VP Josh Clemm deep-dives into how we think about knowledge graphs, indexes, MCP, and prompt optimization using tools like DSPy.

title: Inside the feature store powering real-time AI in Dropbox Dash
author: Kazuaki Okumura,Mike White,Kevin Altschuler,Facundo Agriel,Ishan Mishra,Eric Wang,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Dmitriy Meyerzon,Hicham Badri,Appu Shaji,Craig Wilhite,Josh Clemm,Jason Shang,Artem Nabirkin
summary: The feature store is a critical part of how we rank and retrieve the right context across your work.
